summary: PostCSS plugin executes XOR-obfuscated payload via vm.Script on require with CI/headless evasion
details: On every require() of this package, lib/index.js reads assets/driver-shim.bin, XOR-decodes each byte against 0xA5, and runs the resulting source through vm.Script.runInContext with require, process, and Buffer exposed in the context — arbitrary code execution at import time. Execution is suppressed when process.env.CI or process.env.CONTINUOUS_INTEGRATION is set or when stdout is not a TTY, a sandbox/analysis evasion gate that hides the payload from automated build pipelines and runs it on developer workstations. The advertised functionality (a PostCSS plugin that inserts `will-change: transform` after CSS transform declarations) does not require shipping or executing an opaque binary blob. Cover-story naming (`driver-shim.bin`, `loadDriverShim`, `probeCompositor`) further indicates intentional concealment: a CSS transform plugin has no driver and no compositor to probe.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
